stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
You are flying a heading of 212° and you have correctly tuned and identified two radio navigation aids. The RMI is shown in the annex. You get an updated weather report that warns of a thunderstorm along your route on a relative bearing of approximately 350°. If you continue on your current heading, what will be the effect on the RMI indications?

options.a: Both needles will continue to indicate correctly.
options.b: The ADF needle will start to point to a bearing between 020° and 130°.
options.c: The VOR needle will start to point to a bearing between 135° and 225°.
options.d: The ADF needle will start to point to a bearing between 135° and 225°.
